skill.name: agent-browser
skill.description: Controls a browser through the agent-browser CLI for navigation, form interaction, screenshots, recordings, authentication workflows, and page inspection.
skill.summary: Automate browser navigation, testing, capture, and authenticated web workflows with compact page snapshots.
skill.author: vercel-labs

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, proxy, filesystem, and credential-related patterns. Review confirmed these are mostly documented agent-browser commands and shell templates for legitimate browser automation, but the skill intentionally handles cookies, storage, auth state, proxies, JavaScript evaluation, screenshots, PDFs, and local output files. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious exfiltration was found, so publication is reasonable with clear warnings.

medium_findings.title: Credential and Session State Handling
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The skill documents cookies, local storage, HTTP basic credentials, and saved authentication state. These are legitimate browser automation features, but saved state can contain session tokens and must be protected.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files directly instruct users to save and load browser authentication state and manage cookies and storage. The documentation also warns that state files contain session tokens, confirming the sensitivity.

medium_findings.title: Arbitrary Browser JavaScript and Broad Navigation
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The skill supports opening http, https, file, about, and data URLs and provides an eval command for JavaScript execution in the browser context. This is expected for automation but can expose local content or execute unsafe page scripts if misused.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited commands are explicit capabilities, not inferred behavior. Legitimate testing use is plausible, so the risk is elevated but not evidence of malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Proxy Routing and Request Interception
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The skill supports HTTP, authenticated, and SOCKS proxies plus browser request routing. This helps geo-testing and corporate access, but can mask traffic paths or expose proxy credentials when used carelessly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Proxy and routing commands are clearly documented, including SOCKS proxies and rotating proxy examples. The surrounding text frames them as testing features, not hidden exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Executable Templates Write Local Artifacts
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The shell templates accept user-provided URLs and output paths, then save screenshots, PDFs, page text, auth-state-derived results, and diagnostic screenshots. The commands are expected, but outputs may contain sensitive page data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The templates are executable and perform browser captures and file writes. They quote variables and do not eval user input, so command injection evidence is limited.

low_findings.title: Documentation Command Blocks Triggered Static Alerts
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. Many external command findings are Markdown examples showing how to call agent-browser. They are not hidden execution logic, although users may copy and run them intentionally.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are fenced documentation examples. They do not execute unless a user or agent intentionally runs the documented CLI commands.

low_findings.title: Placeholder URLs and IPs Are Example Targets
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. Hardcoded URLs and localhost addresses appear in examples for login pages, proxy setup, snapshots, and troubleshooting. No evidence found that the skill sends data to an attacker-controlled endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs use example.com, app.example.com, localhost, or illustrative proxy hosts. They are presented as placeholders and not embedded as covert destinations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static weak-cryptography alerts point to descriptive text and tables, not cryptographic code. No hashing, encryption, or credential transformation code was found in the cited files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose, headings, or filenames, not crypto APIs. This is a high-confidence scanner false positive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Browser JavaScript Evaluation
dangerous_patterns.description: The eval command runs JavaScript in the current browser context. Use only on trusted pages or during controlled testing.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The eval command is explicitly documented and is a known powerful browser automation feature.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authentication State Persistence
dangerous_patterns.description: Saved browser state can include cookies and tokens. Treat state files as secrets and avoid committing them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly saves state files and warns that they contain session tokens.

dangerous_patterns.title: Proxy Configuration Including SOCKS
dangerous_patterns.description: Proxy support can route browser traffic through external infrastructure. Validate proxy ownership and avoid embedding credentials in URLs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: SOCKS and authenticated proxy examples are documented directly. The capability is legitimate but security-sensitive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Capture Templates Store Page Data
dangerous_patterns.description: The capture template stores screenshots, structure, text, and PDFs from target pages. Outputs may contain private or regulated information.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The script writes multiple browser artifacts to the output directory. Sensitivity depends on the target page content.

content.user_title: Automate Browser Workflows with Agent Browser
content.value_statement: Manual browser testing and web capture can be slow and hard to repeat. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured CLI workflow for navigation, interaction, capture, and authentication reuse.
actual_capabilities: Navigate to web pages and inspect compact snapshots with element references.
Click, type, fill forms, select options, upload files, scroll, and drag elements.
Capture screenshots, PDFs, videos, traces, console output, and page errors.
Save and load cookies, local storage, and authentication state for repeat sessions.
Configure viewport, devices, geolocation, headers, credentials, proxies, and offline mode.
Use executable templates for form automation, authenticated sessions, and content capture.
limitations: The skill depends on the agent-browser CLI being installed and available.
Element references can change after navigation or dynamic page updates.
Authentication workflows require user-provided credentials and careful state-file handling.
Proxy, JavaScript evaluation, file URL, and capture features require security review before sensitive use.

use_cases.title: End-to-end web checks
use_cases.description: Run repeatable browser checks, capture evidence, and inspect failures with screenshots, videos, traces, and console output.

use_cases.title: Authenticated workflow automation
use_cases.description: Log in once, save browser state, and reuse the session to test protected pages or internal tools.

use_cases.title: Structured web capture
use_cases.description: Open target pages, extract visible text, save page structure, and produce screenshots or PDFs for review.

prompt_templates.title: Open and inspect a page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use agent-browser to open the target URL, take an interactive snapshot, summarize the visible structure, and identify the main actions available.

prompt_templates.title: Fill a form safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Use agent-browser to inspect this form, ask before submitting, fill only the fields I approve, and capture the result page after submission.

prompt_templates.title: Record a failing workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use agent-browser to start a recording, reproduce the workflow failure, collect console errors and page errors, then stop recording and summarize evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Reuse an authenticated session
prompt_templates.prompt: Use agent-browser to load the saved auth state, verify the session is still valid, test the protected workflow, and avoid printing secrets or token values.

output_examples.input: Inspect a login page and identify the fields.
output: Found email and password fields plus a sign-in button.
Recommended taking a new snapshot after submitting because references may change.

output_examples.input: Capture a product page for review.
output: Saved a full-page screenshot and PDF.
Reported the current page title, URL, and main interactive elements.
Identified the failing step and saved visual evidence for review.

best_practices: Take a fresh interactive snapshot before each important interaction.
Treat auth state, cookies, screenshots, PDFs, and recordings as sensitive artifacts.
Use scoped sessions and trusted URLs when testing authenticated or internal sites.
anti_patterns: Do not commit saved browser state or captured private page data.
Do not run JavaScript evaluation on untrusted pages without a clear reason.
Do not route traffic through unknown proxies or embed proxy credentials in shared commands.

faq.question: What does this skill automate?
faq.answer: It automates browser navigation, element interaction, screenshots, PDFs, video recording, page inspection, and authenticated sessions through agent-browser.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Why are element references important?
faq.answer: References let the agent act on compact snapshot IDs instead of parsing full page HTML for every step.

faq.question: Can it handle login flows?
faq.answer: Yes. It can fill login forms, save browser state, reload saved state, and support manual two-factor steps.

faq.question: What security care is required?
faq.answer: Protect saved state files, cookies, screenshots, PDFs, recordings, proxy credentials, and any captured page content.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for public marketplace use?
faq.answer: It has medium risk because it controls browsers and handles sensitive state, but no malicious behavior was confirmed.
